text:
Artificial intelligence is evolving rapidly. Today, most AI systems are "narrow," meaning they excel at specific tasks like language translation or image recognition. However, many experts predict the development of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) within the next few decades. AGI would be a machine that can understand, learn, and perform any intellectual task as well as a human.

The potential benefits of AGI are enormous—it could help solve climate change, cure diseases, and improve education. Yet there are also serious risks, such as job displacement and ethical concerns about control. While some optimists believe AGI will arrive by 2040, others argue it may take much longer. Either way, careful planning is essential to ensure this powerful technology benefits humanity.
